name: Basenji
description: The Basenji is an ancient hunting dog from central Africa, where the Azande and Mangbetu peoples of the Congo basin have used them for millennia to flush game into nets and hunt independently in the forest undergrowth. Famous as the barkless dog, Basenjis produce a unique yodeling vocalization called a baroo rather than a conventional bark, due to the unusually shaped larynx. Their cat-like cleanliness — they groom themselves meticulously — combined with a strong prey drive, independent intelligence, and tendency to selective hearing makes them simultaneously fascinating and challenging companions.

conditions_detail.name: Fanconi Syndrome
conditions_detail.description: Fanconi syndrome is a hereditary proximal renal tubular dysfunction causing abnormal loss of glucose, amino acids, electrolytes, and water into the urine. It leads to a characteristic symptom complex and can cause severe metabolic disturbance.
symptoms: Glucose in urine despite normal blood glucose
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Oral supplementation of the lost electrolytes (sodium bicarbonate, potassium, phosphate) and vitamins. Regular monitoring of blood and urine biochemistry. There is no cure but affected dogs can live for many years with good management.
prevention_tips: DNA test available for Basenji form
Test all breeding Basenjis before mating

conditions_detail.name: Progressive Retinal Atrophy (PRA)
conditions_detail.description: PRA is a group of hereditary diseases causing progressive degeneration of the photoreceptor cells in the retina. It leads to night blindness first, followed by complete blindness. There is no treatment, but DNA tests exist for many forms.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: There is no effective treatment or cure for PRA. Management focuses on adapting the dog's environment to maintain quality of life as vision deteriorates.
prevention_tips: DNA test breeding stock before mating
Only breed clear or carrier-to-clear pairings

conditions_detail.name: Hypothyroidism
conditions_detail.description: Hypothyroidism is the most common endocrine disorder in dogs, caused by reduced thyroid hormone production — most often from immune-mediated thyroiditis or idiopathic follicular atrophy. It affects metabolism, skin, and coat quality.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Daily oral thyroxine (levothyroxine) supplementation is highly effective and required for life. Dosing is adjusted based on thyroid level monitoring every 6-12 months.
prevention_tips: Regular blood screening in at-risk breeds from age 4

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring
